extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won stage 6 from eighth position with 200m to go, surging outside after SABATINI Fabio's tempo control strung out the sprint.
tactical_patterns: SABATINI Fabio controlled tempo while pulling off rather than traditional leadout, stringing peloton to suit KITTEL's late power surge.
Quickstep controlled finale until BOASSON HAGEN Edvald for Dimension Data attacked final kilometer, creating chaos favoring late launchers.
Two roundabouts inside final kilometer stretched peloton and paradoxically reduced crash risk versus straight boulevard finish.
KITTEL launched sprint from eighth wheel at 200m, demonstrating elite sprinters with highest wattage can afford latest timing.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Two wins from three bunch sprints suggests dominance when leadout is even partially functional, favorite for 5+ stage wins
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: neutral: Losing 13 points despite second place shows he cannot match KITTEL's power even when positioning well
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: Lead-out man HALLER Marco protested finish tactics, suggesting team struggling with positioning execution under pressure
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: Commentators identify him as green jersey threat via breakaway points in medium mountain stages, not pure sprints
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: positive: Dimension Data's late-race aggression in final kilometer disrupted Quickstep control, tactical shift after SAGAN absence
